science.title: Breakthrough discovery reveals hidden oxygen flow deep inside catalysts
science.summary: A major discovery is reshaping how scientists think about catalysts. Researchers have, for the first time, captured oxygen atoms moving through the interior of a catalyst—not just along its surface. This reveals that the bulk material can actively participate in reactions, opening a new frontier in catalyst design. The

science.title: This new camera captures what happens in a trillionth of a second
science.summary: Scientists have unveiled a breakthrough imaging method that can capture the hidden details of events unfolding in trillionths of a second. This new technique doesn’t just track how bright something is—it also reveals subtle structural changes that were previously invisible, all in a single shot. By effectively turning

science.title: These tiny dinosaur fossils fooled scientists for 20 years
science.summary: Tiny dinosaur fossils that puzzled scientists for over 20 years have finally revealed their true identity. Rather than belonging to a miniature species, they are actually baby ankylosaurs—some less than a year old, including a possible hatchling. By studying bone growth patterns, researchers confirmed these young dinos

science.title: 95% of people carry this virus and scientists may have just found how to stop it
science.summary: Scientists have taken a major step toward stopping Epstein-Barr virus (EBV), an extremely common infection linked to cancer and chronic disease. science.title: Your phone's next speed boost may come from a strange magnetic jump that rewrites how chips handle heat
science.summary: A new technology has been proposed that could fundamentally solve the issue of smartphones overheating during high-spec gaming or extended video streaming. Researchers at KAIST have discovered the principle of processing signals using the minute vibrations of magnets (spin waves) instead of electrons. This method signi

tech.title: Inventor recalls eye imaging breakthrough
tech.summary: If you’ve been to an eye doctor and had an image taken of the inside of your eye, chances are good it was done with optical coherence tomography (OCT)—a technology invented by clinician-scientist David Huang ’85, SM ’89, PhD ’93, and now used in 40 million procedures per year.  OCT is a noninvasive technique used&

tech.title: Anthropic gets $5B investment from Amazon, will use it to buy Amazon chips
tech.summary: Anthropic secures 5 gigawatts of Amazon’s custom silicon as Claude demand soars.

tech.title: AMD Ryzen 9 9950X3D2 Dual Edition review: Tons of cache for tons of dollars
tech.summary: There are some practical benefits to this $899 chip, but not many.

tech.title: YouTube expands its AI likeness detection technology to celebrities
tech.summary: YouTube is expanding its AI likeness detection tool to celebrities, giving talent and their reps a way to find and remove deepfakes.

tech.title: X makes it 1,900 percent more expensive to post links
tech.summary: Posting links to X through custom social media software just got a lot more expensive. On Monday, X significantly increased how much it costs to post a URL via the X API, which is what third-party tools use to hook into the platform. It now costs $0.20 when a link is posted, up from $0.01. […]

medicine.title: Why 'brain fog' in Parkinson's isn't always what it seems
medicine.summary: For many individuals living with Parkinson's disease and those in its "prodromal" or early stages before movement impairment, there is a distressing gap between how they feel their brain is working and what clinical tests show. Patients often report, "I feel like my thinking is getting worse," only to be told their obj

medicine.title: This cheap drug could help people with type 1 diabetes use less insulin
medicine.summary: A surprising new clinical trial has revealed that metformin—a cheap, century-old drug widely used for type 2 diabetes—may help people with type 1 diabetes in an unexpected way. While researchers initially hoped it would reduce insulin resistance, they instead found it allows patients to use about 12% less insulin while

medicine.title: Forget daily pills. This shot works when blood pressure meds fail
medicine.summary: A twice-yearly injection may soon change how high blood pressure is treated. In a global trial, patients receiving the experimental drug zilebesiran alongside standard therapy saw greater blood pressure reductions than those on standard treatment alone. The drug works by blocking a key liver protein, helping blood vess

medicine.title: How aggressive breast cancer turns off the immune system
medicine.summary: Researchers are launching a new project to crack the mystery of aggressive breast cancer, where predicting disease progression remains a major hurdle. By studying how tumors interact with and suppress the immune system, scientists aim to identify new biomarkers that reveal how the cancer evolves. Using real patient sam

mathematics.title: In Math, Rigor Is Vital. But Are Digitized Proofs Taking It Too Far?
mathematics.summary: The quest to make mathematics rigorous has a long and spotty history — one mathematicians can learn from as they push to formalize everything in the computer program Lean. The post In Math, Rigor Is Vital. But Are Digitized Proofs Taking It Too Far? first appeared on Quanta Magazine

mathematics.title: MIT affiliates win AI for Math grants to accelerate mathematical discovery
mathematics.summary: Department of Mathematics researchers David Roe and Andrew Sutherland seek to advance automated theorem proving; four additional MIT alumni also awarded.

mathematics.title: The Math That Explains Why Bell Curves Are Everywhere
mathematics.summary: The central limit theorem started as a bar trick for 18th-century gamblers. Now scientists rely on it every day. The post The Math That Explains Why Bell Curves Are Everywhere first appeared on Quanta Magazine

mathematics.title: New Strides Made on Deceptively Simple ‘Lonely Runner’ Problem
mathematics.summary: A straightforward conjecture about runners moving around a track turns out to be equivalent to many complex mathematical questions. Three new proofs mark the first significant progress on the problem in decades. The post New Strides Made on Deceptively Simple ‘Lonely Runner’ Problem first appeared on Quanta Magazine

mathematics.title: School of Science welcomed new faculty in 2024
mathematics.summary: Eleven new professors join the departments of Biology; Brain and Cognitive Sciences; Earth, Atmospheric and Planetary Sciences; Mathematics; and Physics.

space.title: NASA at SXSW: Johnson Director Vanessa Wyche on Why Artemis Changes Everything
space.summary: On March 21, 2026, NASA’s Johnson Space Center Director Vanessa Wyche took the stage at the Space House event at South by Southwest in Austin, Texas, to outline NASA’s next giant leap in human spaceflight — from low Earth orbit to the Moon, and ultimately Mars.  As NASA prepares for a new

space.title: What Are Ames’ Contributions to Artemis II?
space.summary: NASA successfully sent four astronauts around the Moon for the first time in more than 50 years, setting the stage for future lunar landing missions. As the agency continues to push the bounds of space exploration, NASA’s Ames Research Center in California’s Silicon Valley provided essential su

space.title: You've heard of the Space Force. Now there's a 'NASA Force' — but it's not about making space war
space.summary: NASA Force aims to recruit industry talent to the space agency for relatively short stints. Applications are open now in the wake of the Artemis 2 moon mission.

space.title: 600 rocket landings! SpaceX notches another milestone during Sunday Starlink launch (video)
space.summary: A SpaceX Falcon 9 rocket carrying 25 Starlink satellites lifted off from Vandenberg Space Force Station in California on April 19. The mission featured the 600th landing of a Falcon first stage.

space.title: SpaceX launches advanced GPS satellite for US Space Force after rocket swap (video, photos)
space.summary: SpaceX launched a new GPS satellite for the U.S. Space Force early Tuesday morning (April 21) — a mission that was originally supposed to fly on a competitor's rocket.

astronomy.title: NASA’s SPHEREx Telescope Just Mapped the Cosmic Ices That Will Someday Build Planets
astronomy.summary: New missions mean new capabilities - and one particularly interesting new mission is finally up and running. Data is starting to come in from SPHEREx, the medium-class surveyor that is mapping the entire sky every six months. A paper based on some of that early data was recently published in The Astrophysical Journal,

astronomy.title: Planets Collide Around Young, Sun-like Star
astronomy.summary: Astronomers have uncovered evidence that two planets collided around a young star, revealing how giant impacts sculpt baby solar systems. The post Planets Collide Around Young, Sun-like Star appeared first on Sky & Telescope.

astronomy.title: How a Black Hole and a Shredded Star Could Light Up a Galaxy
astronomy.summary: In 2014, a strange cloudy object called G2 made a close approach to Sagittarius A*, (Sag A*) the supermassive black hole at the heart of the Milky Way Galaxy. Astronomers were pretty excited, partly because they thought it might get torn apart by Sag A*'s intense gravitational pull. That didn't happen, and the event wa

astronomy.title: Astronomers Find the Edge of the Milky Way
astronomy.summary: Astronomers have located the edge of the Milky Way’s star-forming disk for the first time, showing that star formation is focused within 40,000 light-years of our galactic center. The post Astronomers Find the Edge of the Milky Way appeared first on Sky & Telescope.

astronomy.title: Rubin Observatory Announces 11,000 New Asteroids
astronomy.summary: The Vera C. Rubin Observatory will discover up to 500,000 solar system objects every year. It’s already starting to deliver on that promise. The post Rubin Observatory Announces 11,000 New Asteroids appeared first on Sky & Telescope.

architecture.title: Jingu Studio / YNAS
architecture.summary: The renovation of Villa Serena 204 began as a personal and professional mission to challenge Japan's "scrap-and-build" culture. architecture.title: Limberlost Place / Moriyama Teshima Architects
architecture.summary: Limberlost Place, on Toronto's waterfront, is perhaps the world's first public tall timber building—a technical landmark that also elevates the art of architecture. architecture.title: Gensler set to design Trump Tower Tbilisi as country's tallest building
architecture.summary: The Trump Organization has announced plans to build a 70-storey skyscraper in Tbilisi, Georgia, which would be the tallest building in the country. architecture.title: Five Texas cities "catalyzing" urban development with convention centres
architecture.summary: Nearly $9 billion of convention centre design and construction is currently underway in Texas's five biggest cities. Local writer Kate Mazade asks experts about the phenomenon and dives into the plans for each city. architecture.title: Flower-covered theatre forms centrepiece of Denise Scott Brown's photography exhibition
architecture.summary: A flower-covered theatre has been installed at the centre of an exhibition dedicated to architect Denise Scott Brown's photographs at the brutalist Yale architecture school building in New Haven, USA. construction.title: $1B Virginia airport upgrade project reaches milestone
construction.summary: W.M. Jordan served as construction manager at risk for Norfolk International Airport’s three new gates in Concourse A, an addition spanning nearly 19,000 square feet.

construction.title: 3 megaprojects triggered rebound in March construction starts
construction.summary: Starts in electric power and utilities leapt over 353% month over month, according to Dodge, even as data centers decelerated.

construction.title: California contractor ordered to pay $468K in wage theft case
construction.summary: A federal probe found missed payroll, unpaid overtime and retaliation, highlighting persistent labor violations in construction, according to an attorney.

ai.title: The World Leaks the Future: Harness Evolution for Future Prediction Agents
ai.summary: arXiv:2604.15719v2 Announce Type: new Abstract: Many consequential decisions must be made before the relevant outcome is known. Such problems are commonly framed as future prediction, where an LLM agent must form a prediction for an unresolved question using only the public information available at the prediction time.
